meta.seed_scenario: One person made a joke at the other’s expense in public, leading to embarrassment and hurt, even after being asked to stop.
meta.selected_scenario: One neighbor makes a light-hearted comment about the other's yard work, but it comes off as condescending, causing irritation.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Hey Trinh, nice job on the yard! I didn’t know you could pull weeds that fast!

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Uh, thanks, I guess. I just... wanted it to look decent.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Right, but I thought you’d be out there all day. You know, getting your steps in.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Yeah, well, not everyone takes a whole Saturday to do yard work, Jack.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Oh come on, I was just joking. Lighten up a bit!

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: It didn’t sound like a joke. It sounded kinda condescending.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I didn’t mean it like that. Just thought it was impressive.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Impressive? Or just surprising?

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Surprising? No! I mean, I just didn’t expect you to finish so quick.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Well, maybe next time think about how it comes off, okay?

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: Someone is attempting to meditate in their room, but the other brings in friends for a raucous game night, disrupting the calm.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Hey, can you keep it down a bit? I'm trying to meditate.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Oh, come on! It's game night. We're just starting a new round.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: I get that, but I've been really needing some quiet time. It’s a bit much.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Just for a couple of hours. Can’t you just, I don’t know, join us for a bit?

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: I don’t want to join. I want to focus. Can you just... keep it down?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I’m not being that loud! Besides, it’s not every day we have friends over.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Right, but I live here too, you know? Just trying to catch a break.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Fine, I’ll lower my voice. But you can’t expect silence forever.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Yeah, I know. Just trying to recharge for a bit.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Alright, I’ll keep it down. Just don't be too long, okay?

meta.seed_scenario: One person initiates physical affection or intimacy, and the other pulls away or makes them feel ashamed for wanting it.
meta.selected_scenario: One person expresses a desire for more intimacy, while the other responds with indifference or a dismissive comment about their needs.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Hey, can we talk for a minute? I feel like we haven't really connected lately.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Do we have to? I mean, I'm kinda busy with this game.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I know, but I just... I miss us, you know? Like, I was hoping we could maybe spend some time together later.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Later? Like after the game? You know how I get into it.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Yeah, but it feels like you're always into it. Can’t we take a break and just... I don’t know, cuddle or something?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Cuddle? Right now? It’s the playoffs, Emma.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I’m not asking for much. Just some time with you. I feel like I’m asking for a lot here.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: It’s not that big a deal. You always want that when I’m busy.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: It’s not like I’m trying to ruin your fun. I just want to feel... close.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Can’t it wait? Just one game?

meta.seed_scenario: Someone feels excluded when decisions are made in a shared group without consulting them, despite their ongoing involvement.
meta.selected_scenario: One opponent makes unilateral decisions about a shared project, leaving the other feeling sidelined despite their contributions.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: So, I noticed you went ahead and changed the project deadline without even bringing it up in the last meeting.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: I thought it was kind of obvious we needed to speed things up. We’re behind schedule.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: But you didn't even ask anyone. I’ve been working on stuff based on the old timeline.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: I’m sure you can adjust. It’s not the end of the world.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: It feels like you’re just making these calls on your own. Like my input doesn’t matter.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: Well, someone had to take charge. It’s not like you were stepping up.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: I’m involved, Jessica. I’ve been putting in hours, and you know that.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: Yeah, but you’re not the only one with a say in this. I have to think about the bigger picture.

script.speaker: Michael
script.text: And I just get sidelined while you do that? It’s frustrating.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: I can’t run everything past you every time. We need decisions made.

meta.seed_scenario: One person dismisses or mocks the other's long-term aspirations, suggesting they are unrealistic or unimportant.
meta.selected_scenario: One person expresses excitement about a new opportunity, but the other responds with skepticism, suggesting it might be a waste of time.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Hey, Trinh, did you hear about that grant program for startups? I’m thinking of applying.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Oh, really? You think that's a good idea? Seems like a lot of work for something that might not even go anywhere.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I mean, it could lead to something big! I have this concept that could really take off.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I get that, but there are a ton of people applying. What makes you think you’ll stand out?

script.speaker: David
script.text: I’ve got a unique angle! It’s not just another tech app. I’ve put a lot of thought into it.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Yeah, but you said the same about that last project. Remember how that fizzled out?

script.speaker: David
script.text: That was different! I learned from that. This time, I’m way more prepared.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I just don’t want to see you waste your time again, you know? It’s not easy out there.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I appreciate your concern, but I need to try. I can't just keep playing it safe.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Fine, but don’t come crying to me if it doesn’t pan out. You know I’ll be busy with my own projects.

meta.seed_scenario: One person goes through a difficult time but feels the other isn’t showing up—whether emotionally, practically, or with their presence.
meta.selected_scenario: A subordinate seeks guidance during a challenging project but feels their superior is too busy to provide meaningful support.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Hey David, do you have a minute? I’m struggling with the Thompson presentation.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I’m a bit swamped right now. Can it wait?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I just... I really need some input on the data analysis section. I thought we could brainstorm together.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I get that, but I have back-to-back meetings all day. Can you send me an email with your questions?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I could, but it’s kind of complicated. I was hoping to talk it through.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Look, I can’t drop everything for this right now. You’re capable, Trinh. Just try to work through it.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I am capable, but I really thought you'd be more involved. It feels like I'm on my own.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I know, but you need to manage this. I can’t hold your hand on every project.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Fine. I’ll do my best. Just... let me know when you’re free.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Sure, just keep me posted.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone refuses to take basic health precautions, causing the other to feel unsafe.
meta.selected_scenario: An elder advises a child to be cautious about a new hobby, but the child dismisses the advice as overprotective.

script.speaker: Ben
script.text: I just got this skateboard, Aunt Susan! It’s so cool.

script.speaker: Susan
script.text: That's great, Ben, but you need to be careful. Did you get a helmet?

script.speaker: Ben
script.text: Nah, it's fine. I’ll just go really slow.

script.speaker: Susan
script.text: Slow or not, you really should wear something. I don’t want you getting hurt.

script.speaker: Ben
script.text: But everyone at the park doesn’t wear helmets. They’re fine.

script.speaker: Susan
script.text: That doesn’t mean you should follow them. What if you fall? It’s just a precaution.

script.speaker: Ben
script.text: I can handle it! You’re being too overprotective.

script.speaker: Susan
script.text: I’m just looking out for you. You know how clumsy you can be.

script.speaker: Ben
script.text: Come on! I’m not a kid anymore. Just let me have fun.

script.speaker: Susan
script.text: I want you to have fun, but it’s not fun if you’re hurt.

meta.seed_scenario: One person continues a behavior that affects the other negatively, even after being asked to change.
meta.selected_scenario: One partner tries to communicate their need for space, but the other insists on being together, disregarding their feelings.

script.speaker: Thuy
script.text: Can we just, I don't know, have a night in? I really need some space.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: But I was looking forward to our date night. We haven't had one in ages.

script.speaker: Thuy
script.text: I know, but I’m just feeling overwhelmed. I just want to be alone for a bit.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Alone? Like, really? You can’t just shut me out like that.

script.speaker: Thuy
script.text: I’m not shutting you out. I just need... a break. It’s been a lot lately.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: So, what am I supposed to do? Just sit at home while you’re off... doing whatever?

script.speaker: Thuy
script.text: It’s not like that! I just need to recharge. Can’t you understand?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I get it, but can’t we just spend a little time together first?

script.speaker: Thuy
script.text: I’m really not in the mood for that. I just want to be in my own head for a while.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Fine, but this isn’t how it should be. I miss you.

meta.seed_scenario: One person disrupts the other’s sleep with noise, lights, or inconsiderate timing, even after being asked not to.
meta.selected_scenario: One person makes plans that disrupt the other's schedule, despite knowing they prefer a quieter evening.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Liam, could you keep it down a bit? I was just starting to doze off.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Oh, sorry! I didn't realize I was being that loud. I thought you liked this song.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I do, but I wanted a quiet night. Remember, I mentioned I had a long day?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Yeah, but I just figured we'd have some fun. It’s the weekend, right?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Fun is great, but I really need to recharge. It’s just... frustrating.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Alright, I get it. But can’t we compromise? I’ll turn it down.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Compromise would be not blasting music at all. I just wanted to relax.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Fine. I’ll stop the music. But you have to promise we’ll do something fun tomorrow.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Okay, deal. Just... let’s try to stick to quieter evenings, yeah?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Yeah, I’ll try. Sorry again.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone shares something they found uplifting, and the other responds with cynicism or mockery, dulling the moment.
meta.selected_scenario: Someone opens up about a recent struggle, and the other deflects by making a joke, shifting the focus away from the serious conversation.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: So, I finally talked to my boss about that promotion. It was kinda nerve-wracking.

script.speaker: Nia
script.text: Oh? Did you practice your speech in front of the mirror like you said you would?

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Well, I meant to, but then I just... didn’t. Anyway, it went okay, I guess.

script.speaker: Nia
script.text: You guess? Come on, you’ve been stressing about this for weeks!

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Yeah, but he just told me to keep doing what I’m doing. That’s not exactly a glowing review.

script.speaker: Nia
script.text: That’s like saying you’re good enough but not quite good enough. Classic.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Exactly! I feel like I just wasted a whole day worrying.

script.speaker: Nia
script.text: Hey, at least you didn’t trip over your own feet while you were talking.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Nia, that’s not really helpful right now.

script.speaker: Nia
script.text: I’m just saying, it could be worse. You could be me trying to figure out my life choices.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone tries to offer 'helpful' advice by steering the other person toward a path they didn’t choose, assuming they know what’s best.
meta.selected_scenario: One sibling expresses excitement about a personal project, but the other dismisses it as a waste of time, insisting they should pursue something more conventional.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: I finally started working on that app idea I told you about. It’s gonna be great!

script.speaker: Trang
script.text: An app? Really? What, another one of those 'fun projects'?

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: No, it’s different this time. I actually have a plan.

script.speaker: Trang
script.text: A plan? You mean just more late nights and junk food?

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Come on, it’s not like that. I need to try something new.

script.speaker: Trang
script.text: But what about your internship? You could be using your time on something that’ll actually get you a job.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: This could lead to a job. It’s about building my skills.

script.speaker: Trang
script.text: Or it could just end up as another dead end. Why not focus on something stable?

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Because that’s not what I want! Can't you just support me for once?

script.speaker: Trang
script.text: I just don’t want you to waste your time, that’s all.

meta.seed_scenario: During arguments, one person raises their voice or uses sarcasm, making the other feel unsafe or emotionally shut down.
meta.selected_scenario: During a discussion about responsibilities, one person raises their voice, causing the other to withdraw and feel unheard.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Jack, can you please just put your laundry in the basket? It’s not that hard.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I was going to do it later, Mom.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Later? You've been saying that for days. I can't keep reminding you every time.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I get it, but you don’t need to yell about it.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I’m not yelling! I'm just frustrated.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Well, it sounds like yelling to me. Can’t we just have a normal conversation?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: A normal conversation? You mean like the ones where I do all the talking and you just ignore me?

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I’m not ignoring you! I just don’t see what the big deal is.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: The big deal is I asked you to help out around here. It’s not just my job to keep this place clean!

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Fine! I’ll do it. Just stop with the tone, okay?

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: Someone is attempting to have a serious conversation about project timelines, but the other diverts the topic to personal anecdotes, ignoring the urgency.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: So, about the project timeline—I'm really concerned we might miss the deadline.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: Oh, did I tell you about the weekend? I went hiking, and it was just beautiful. You’d love the view!

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: That sounds nice, but we need to focus on the schedule right now.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: Right, right, but I figured sharing a bit might lighten the mood. You seemed pretty stressed.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I get that, but this is urgent. If we don’t sort out these timelines, we’re in trouble.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: Well, we can handle it! I mean, I do have some ideas for the presentation that could fit in.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: We can brainstorm later! I just need you to stay on track for a minute.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: Okay, okay, let me just finish this thought—

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: No, Mei, I really need to know if you can meet that deadline.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: Fine, I will make it work. Just... remind me after we talk about the hike.

meta.seed_scenario: One person made a joke at the other’s expense in public, leading to embarrassment and hurt, even after being asked to stop.
meta.selected_scenario: One neighbor offers unsolicited advice on home improvements, but the other feels their choices are being criticized rather than supported.

script.speaker: Thomas
script.text: Hey Marisol, I noticed you were painting your shutters. I think a darker color would really pop.

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: Oh, uh, yeah. I kind of like this blue. It matches the house.

script.speaker: Thomas
script.text: Sure, but you know, the dark navy is really in right now. Just saying.

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: Right, but I want it to look nice for me, you know? Not just what’s trendy.

script.speaker: Thomas
script.text: I get that, but it seems... a bit washed out, you know? Like, it doesn’t pop against the white.

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: I’m not trying to compete with anyone, Thomas. I just want it to feel like home.

script.speaker: Thomas
script.text: I didn’t mean it like that. Just thought I’d help since you asked for opinions before.

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: Yeah, I know. But it feels like you’re not really supporting my choice.

script.speaker: Thomas
script.text: I’m just trying to share ideas. I didn’t mean to upset you.

script.speaker: Marisol
script.text: It’s fine. I’ll just... keep painting, I guess.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: One person tries to unwind after a long day, but the other insists on hosting loud gatherings, disregarding the need for peace.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Hey, do you think we could keep it down a bit? I just got home and I’m trying to unwind.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: Unwind? It's Friday night! I can’t help it if my friends want to hang out.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I get that, but it’s really loud. I can’t even hear myself think over there.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: I mean, it’s just music and laughter. It’s not like we’re throwing a rave or something.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Yeah, but it feels like one. I had a rough week, and I just need some quiet.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: Okay, but it’s just a couple of hours. Can’t you just deal with it?

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I shouldn’t have to deal with it every weekend. Can’t you just finish up early? I’ll even help clean up.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: Fine, I’ll tell them to keep it down. But you owe me for ruining my fun next time!

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Deal. Just… I really need to relax.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: Alright, I’ll make sure it’s more chill.

meta.seed_scenario: One person initiates physical affection or intimacy, and the other pulls away or makes them feel ashamed for wanting it.
meta.selected_scenario: One person attempts to initiate a romantic gesture, but the other reacts with annoyance, suggesting they should focus on more practical matters.

script.speaker: David
script.text: Hey, Jess, come here for a sec.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: What is it? I'm in the middle of sorting these bills.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I know, but I just wanted to show you this thing I found online. It’s... kind of romantic.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: Romantic? Can it wait? We have to figure out this budget.

script.speaker: David
script.text: It'll just take a minute. I thought we could use a little break.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: Yeah, but we can't keep pushing this off. I mean, look at this pile. It's not going to disappear.

script.speaker: David
script.text: I get that, but can't we just have a moment? It feels like we haven't connected in days.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: Well, maybe if you helped more with this stuff, we could connect later.

script.speaker: David
script.text: That’s not fair. I’m trying to make time for us, but you keep brushing it off.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: I’m not brushing it off. I'm just trying to be practical!

meta.seed_scenario: Someone feels excluded when decisions are made in a shared group without consulting them, despite their ongoing involvement.
meta.selected_scenario: In a meeting, one party presents plans that exclude the other, creating tension over the lack of consultation in the decision-making process.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: So, I just saw the new project plan you guys sent out. Not a word about my inputs.

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: I thought you were okay with that direction. You didn't say anything in the last meeting.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Right, but that’s the point. I didn’t know those decisions were being made already. I thought we were still discussing options.

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: Well, we can’t wait forever. We had to move on. It’s not my fault you didn't follow up.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I was busy with my other projects! You know that. You could’ve at least checked in.

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: We all have things going on, Daniel. You think it’s easy to juggle this? You could’ve made time.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: It’s not about time, Tamika. It’s about being kept in the loop. I’m not some afterthought here.

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: If you feel that way, maybe you should be more proactive. This isn’t a high school project.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: That’s rich coming from you. You act like you’re the only one who matters.

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: I can’t help it if you don’t pull your weight. I’m trying to get things done.

meta.seed_scenario: One person dismisses or mocks the other's long-term aspirations, suggesting they are unrealistic or unimportant.
meta.selected_scenario: One person questions the other's commitment to their career goals, implying they are not serious enough about their future.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: So, I heard you want to start a podcast? Like, really?

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: Yeah, I think it could be fun. Why is that surprising?

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I just thought you were all about climbing the corporate ladder. A podcast seems... I don't know, casual.

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: Casual? It's a creative outlet! I can still focus on my career and do something I enjoy.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Sure, but isn't it a little... I don't know, distracting? Like, how committed are you to your job if you're doing this?

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: Wow, really? You think I can’t juggle both? That's kind of condescending.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I’m just saying, maybe prioritize the promotion first? This podcast thing feels like a hobby.

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: A hobby that's part of my long-term goals, Daniel. But you wouldn't get it.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I don't get why you'd risk your focus on something... niche.

script.speaker: Tamika
script.text: Niche? It’s about storytelling, and that matters to me. Sorry if that makes you uncomfortable.

meta.seed_scenario: One person goes through a difficult time but feels the other isn’t showing up—whether emotionally, practically, or with their presence.
meta.selected_scenario: One person shares their struggles with workload, but the other brushes it off, suggesting they should just manage better.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: Hey, Daniel, can we talk for a minute? I'm feeling really swamped with this project.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Sure, but you know we’ve got deadlines. Just prioritize better.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: I get that, but it's more than just prioritizing. The workload's gotten really heavy.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Everyone's busy, Aaliyah. We all have to manage.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: I’m not asking for a break. Just some support, maybe? Like, if you could help with the reports this week?

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I can't just drop what I'm doing. You need to figure this out yourself.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: That’s easy for you to say. You’re not the one drowning in the details.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I’m not trying to be unsympathetic. I just... have my own stuff to handle.

script.speaker: Aaliyah
script.text: It feels like I'm on my own here, Daniel. Just a little backup would go a long way.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I’ll see what I can do, but you really need to keep it together.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone refuses to take basic health precautions, causing the other to feel unsafe.
meta.selected_scenario: A child wants to try a new sport without protective gear, while the elder insists on safety measures, leading to a disagreement.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I don't need a helmet, Aunt Sandra! I’ll be fine.

script.speaker: Sandra
script.text: You just fell off your skateboard last week, remember? Put it on.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: That was different! I was going too fast.

script.speaker: Sandra
script.text: And now you're going to try surfing? You’re crazy if you think I’m letting you do that without protection.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: But everyone else doesn’t wear one! I’ll look dumb.

script.speaker: Sandra
script.text: Looking dumb is better than getting hurt. You can’t just ignore safety because of what others do.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I just want to try it, that’s all! You’re being overprotective.

script.speaker: Sandra
script.text: I’m being smart. You might think you’re invincible, but trust me, accidents happen.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Fine, I’ll wear it… but I’m still gonna be the coolest out there.

script.speaker: Sandra
script.text: Cool or not, just stay safe, okay?

meta.seed_scenario: One person continues a behavior that affects the other negatively, even after being asked to change.
meta.selected_scenario: After one person expresses discomfort with a recurring behavior, the other brushes it off, insisting it's not a big deal.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Can you turn that down a bit? It's hard to focus.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: It's just background music, Wei. It’s not that loud.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Yeah, but I can still hear it. I thought we agreed you'd keep it quieter when I'm working?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I remember saying I’d try, but this is just... it’s my vibe right now.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Your vibe? I get it, but we’ve talked about this. It distracts me.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I know, but it’s not like I’m blaring it. You’re being a bit dramatic.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: Dramatic? I’m just asking you to be considerate for once.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I am being considerate! I let you have your quiet time the rest of the day.

script.speaker: Wei
script.text: That’s not the point. It feels like you’re brushing off what I need.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I’m not brushing it off! Just... can we compromise? I’ll keep it low, but I need this.

meta.seed_scenario: One person disrupts the other’s sleep with noise, lights, or inconsiderate timing, even after being asked not to.
meta.selected_scenario: After one partner expresses a need for rest, the other continues to play loud music, claiming it's just for fun.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Could you turn that down a little? I’m trying to get some sleep.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: But it's just a song! It’s not that loud.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: It is loud. I told you earlier I needed to rest.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I know, but I just got into this playlist. It’s fun!

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Yeah, fun for you. I’m the one who has to wake up early.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Just give me a few minutes! I’ll turn it down after this song.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: A few minutes? Seriously? You said that last time.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I’m just trying to enjoy the evening, Emma.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Enjoy it quietly then. I can’t keep doing this every night.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Fine, I’ll stop. Just... can’t you let me have some fun?

meta.seed_scenario: Someone shares something they found uplifting, and the other responds with cynicism or mockery, dulling the moment.
meta.selected_scenario: After a friend expresses excitement about a new hobby, the other quickly compares it to their own past failures, undermining the enthusiasm.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: So, I started this pottery class! It’s like, super relaxing, you know? I made a bowl today.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Nice. I tried that once. Ended up with a lumpy disaster.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: But that’s part of it! It’s not about being perfect. It’s just fun.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Fun until you realize you’re just terrible at it. I mean, why bother?

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: I don’t know, maybe because it’s enjoyable? I was actually excited to show you.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Yeah, well, excitement doesn’t cover the fact that I made a total mess of it.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: Come on, just let loose! It’s not a competition.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Easy for you to say. You’ve already got some skill. I’m just... not cut out for this.

script.speaker: Jessica
script.text: You need to give yourself a break. You’re way too hard on yourself.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Maybe. But I don’t want to waste time on something I’ll just screw up.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone tries to offer 'helpful' advice by steering the other person toward a path they didn’t choose, assuming they know what’s best.
meta.selected_scenario: One sibling offers unsolicited advice about a career choice, assuming they know better, while the other feels their autonomy is being undermined.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: I just got a call from that design firm I applied to. They want to schedule an interview!

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: That's great! But have you thought about going back to school? I mean, a degree could really help.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: I’m not going back to school right now, Jack. I want to start working.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: But you could get a better job if you had more qualifications. It’s just common sense.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: Common sense for you, maybe. I’ve got my plan, and it doesn’t involve more debt.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I’m just trying to help. You don’t want to hit a dead end.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: You think I can’t figure this out? I know what I’m doing.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: You're not seeing the bigger picture. I just want you to be successful.

script.speaker: Mei
script.text: Success looks different for everyone. Let me handle my career.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Fine, but don’t come crying to me when it gets tough.

meta.seed_scenario: During arguments, one person raises their voice or uses sarcasm, making the other feel unsafe or emotionally shut down.
meta.selected_scenario: In a moment of frustration, one person resorts to shouting, making the other feel cornered and reluctant to share their feelings.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Emma, I told you to clean your room before going out. This isn’t the first time.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I was just about to! I can’t do everything at once.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Then what are you doing? Looks like you’re just on your phone.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I was checking something for school! It’s not like you help with anything.

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Help? I work all day, and this is how you repay me?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: It’s not about repaying you! It’s just a mess. I’ll get to it!

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: You always say that! But then it’s the same next week.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Maybe if you didn’t yell every time, I’d feel like I could actually talk to you!

script.speaker: Quoc
script.text: Yelling? I’m just trying to get you to understand how important this is.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Well, it doesn’t feel important when you’re screaming.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: While one colleague is preparing for a presentation, the other engages in loud discussions nearby, failing to recognize the need for concentration.

script.speaker: John
script.text: Trinh, could we maybe tone it down a bit? I’m trying to focus on my presentation.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I get that, but we have to finalize these details before the meeting. It’s kind of urgent.

script.speaker: John
script.text: I understand, but there’s a lot going on over there. It’s hard to concentrate.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Well, I didn’t think my voice was that loud. I’m just trying to keep everyone in the loop.

script.speaker: John
script.text: I know, but I can hear everything. Maybe take it to another room?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: We don’t really have another space. This is the only conference room available.

script.speaker: John
script.text: Can’t you just handle it a bit quieter? Just for a few minutes?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I’ll try, but I can’t promise anything. This is pretty important.

script.speaker: John
script.text: I get that. I just need to get this done, too.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Okay, okay. Let’s see if we can wrap this up quickly.

meta.seed_scenario: One person made a joke at the other’s expense in public, leading to embarrassment and hurt, even after being asked to stop.
meta.selected_scenario: After a neighbor shares a personal story, the other quickly changes the topic to something trivial, leaving the first feeling dismissed.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: So, I was telling you about that time I got stuck in the elevator, right?

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Yeah, yeah. But did you see that new coffee shop opening up? Looks nice.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Uh, yeah, but I was in there for like an hour, Liam. It was kind of scary.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Right, but coffee! I mean, it's like a whole new vibe for the block.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I get that, but come on. I was literally freaking out.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: Sure, but we can talk about that later. The coffee shop is more... you know, urgent.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Urgent? I just wanted to share something that happened to me. It felt pretty important.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: I mean, yeah, but it’s not like it was life or death, right?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I just feel like maybe you didn't care to listen.

script.speaker: Liam
script.text: That’s not it. I just thought you'd want to feel better with some good news.

meta.seed_scenario: Someone seeks a calm environment to recharge, but the other brings in chaos—noise, drama, interruptions—without considering that the other person needs.
meta.selected_scenario: While one roommate is focused on studying, the other plays music at a high volume, ignoring the request for quiet.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Hey, Trinh, can you turn that down a bit? I’m trying to study.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: What? I can’t hear you over the music!

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Exactly. That’s the problem. I’m on a deadline here.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: It’s just a little background music. It helps me focus.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Yeah, but I can’t focus with it blasting. Can’t you use headphones?

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: I was, but they broke. I thought you liked this song.

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: I do, but... it’s just too much right now.

script.speaker: Trinh
script.text: Fine, I’ll turn it down. But you owe me a dance later!

script.speaker: Jack
script.text: Deal. Just let me get through this first.

meta.seed_scenario: One person initiates physical affection or intimacy, and the other pulls away or makes them feel ashamed for wanting it.
meta.selected_scenario: After one partner shares their feelings about wanting to connect, the other makes a joke that trivializes the conversation.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: Hey, can we talk for a minute? I’ve been feeling like we haven’t really connected lately.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Sure. You mean like, connected as in a couple or... connected as in our Wi-Fi needs fixing?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: I’m serious, Daniel. I just want to cuddle or something. It's been a while.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Cuddle? You mean like on the couch while watching another true crime documentary?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: That’s not what I meant. I just want... some intimacy. Like we used to have.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Yeah, but we’re tired, and it’s not like we’re 20 anymore, right?

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: It’s not about age. I just feel like you’re shutting me out.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I’m not shutting you out, I just thought we were both good with how things are.

script.speaker: Emma
script.text: No, I’m not good with it, and I thought you’d want to at least talk about it.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Okay, okay. Let’s talk about it. But can we just keep it light? Like, you know, not too serious?

meta.seed_scenario: Someone feels excluded when decisions are made in a shared group without consulting them, despite their ongoing involvement.
meta.selected_scenario: While discussing strategies, one person dismisses the other's input, insisting on their own approach without consideration for collaboration.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: So, I think we should just go with my plan for the event. It’s straightforward and everyone can follow it.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: Your plan? You didn’t even ask what anyone else thought. I’ve been working on a proposal that fits better with our goals.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: Yeah, but it’s complicated. We need something simple to get everyone on board.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: Complicated doesn’t mean it’s not better. We can’t just dismiss good ideas because they’re not easy.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: It’s not about dismissing, it’s about being practical. We have a deadline.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: And that’s why I brought it up two weeks ago. I thought we were supposed to collaborate.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I didn’t see much action on your part since then. You should’ve pushed harder.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: It’s easy to say that now, Daniel. I’m here, but you keep deciding without me.

script.speaker: Daniel
script.text: I’m just trying to keep this moving. If you want to change things, let’s hear it then.

script.speaker: Maribel
script.text: I can’t just throw ideas into the ring if they’re going to be ignored.
